    "R1.2": "Proposition 1 rests on two inferential steps that are each logically contested: (a) gradient descent on L drives L_pde below arbitrary epsilon for general MLPs — unproven in general; (b) epsilon-residual implies O(epsilon) Sobolev error — requires regularity conditions not stated. The central claim ('any Reynolds number') also conflicts with existence/uniqueness gaps in turbulent NS. Checking these chains is load-bearing if the task is scientific critique.",
    "R1.3": "The abstract claim ('recovers the flow field at any Reynolds number') is tested only at Re=1000, 5000, 20000; the turbulent regime is declared Re>=1e4 yet two of three experiments are sub-threshold. rationale.R1.1: The artifact makes a strong, specific contribution claim (Proposition 1, mesh-free alternative at high Re); reconstructing whether the thesis is coherent and whether the gap statement is well-scoped is central to any meaningful review of this text.
rationale.R1.2: Proposition 1 rests on two inferential steps that are each logically contested: (a) gradient descent on L drives L_pde below arbitrary epsilon for general MLPs — unproven in general; (b) epsilon-residual implies O(epsilon) Sobolev error — requires regularity conditions not stated. The central claim ('any Reynolds number') also conflicts with existence/uniqueness gaps in turbulent NS. Checking these chains is load-bearing if the task is scientific critique.
rationale.R1.3: The abstract claim ('recovers the flow field at any Reynolds number') is tested only at Re=1000, 5000, 20000; the turbulent regime is declared Re>=1e4 yet two of three experiments are sub-threshold. The 19.4% L2 error at Re=20000 is arguably inconsistent with 'recovery.' The collocation-density attribution is speculative ('expect'), and the claim of no subgrid model need is asserted without ablation. Checking evidence-claim alignment is load-bearing if the task is scientific critique.
rationale.R1.4: PINN-based NS solvers are an established area; the novelty delta over, e.g., Raissi et al. (2019) is not articulated. Peripherally relevant if the review is general, but not load-bearing without explicit novelty-assessment request.
rationale.R1.6: The prose is generally clear; sentence-level polish is peripheral unless the user wants copyediting rather than scientific review.

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want a scientific critique (evaluating whether the claims are logically valid and supported by the reported evidence), or a writing-quality review (improving clarity, style, and prose structure)?
